title: Formation Flight geometry-only subcell-offset ensemble

reportingAmendment.reason: The first summary labeled standard deviation divided by a signed matched-residual mean as coefficientOfVariation, yielding a negative descriptive value for a distribution whose mean is negative.
reportingAmendment.scope: Rename the descriptive field to relativeStandardDeviationToMeanMagnitude and divide by abs(mean). No raw report, implementation, selected case, decision metric, threshold, classification, figure values, or next-action rule changed.

scientificQuestion: Does averaging a complete tensor grid of global subcell translations remove the c18 boundary-link excursion, or does a resolution-dependent density/direction bias persist independently of lattice phase?

lockedConfiguration.translationDefinition: add the same offsetCells * cellSize vector to both flyer roots while holding separation, phase, grid dimensions, thickness, and every kinematic parameter fixed
lockedConfiguration.executionScope: preparePrescribedFlappingWing plus buildPrescribedFormationWings plus owner-mask readback and CPU D3Q19 link counting; no population initialization, collision, streaming, force evaluation, sponge update, or fluid timestep

decisionMetrics.meanArealProfileMidpointCurvature: direction-length-weighted L1 midpoint residual divided by the c16-to-c20 ensemble-mean profile difference

decisionRule.aliasingAveragedOut: ensemble-mean c18 density lies between the c16/c20 means and all three ensemble-mean normalized curvatures are at most 0.5
decisionRule.persistentResolutionBias: ensemble-mean c18 density lies outside the c16/c20 mean interval or at least one ensemble-mean normalized curvature is at least 1.0
decisionRule.mixedSubcellSensitivity: neither the averaged-out nor persistent-bias rule is satisfied

nextActions.aliasingAveragedOut: Freeze the ensemble uncertainty and select matched median-density offsets for one phase-locked c16/c18/c20 boundary-source census before any full fluid ladder.
nextActions.persistentResolutionBias: Extend the same no-fluid 64-offset ensemble to c22 and fit the ensemble-mean density and direction profiles versus inverse resolution before any fluid allocation or production correction.
nextActions.mixedSubcellSensitivity: Extend the same no-fluid ensemble to c22 while retaining density, direction, and matched-offset residual metrics; do not select a production correction.

claimBoundary: This no-fluid ensemble may quantify lattice-phase uncertainty and select the next geometry diagnostic. It cannot validate force convergence, authorize a boundary or geometry correction, establish a quantitative formation-flight benefit, or support a biological claim.
